skill.name: grill-me
skill.description: Runs a focused design or planning interview that challenges assumptions, follows decision branches, and asks one question at a time.
skill.summary: Stress-test plans and designs through persistent, structured questioning.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The four static weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by natural-language text in SKILL.md. No cryptographic APIs, executable code, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempts were found. The skill can intentionally inspect the local codebase when relevant, so filesystem access is documented as a low-risk factor.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Description
low_findings.description: The static analyzer raised three duplicate weak-cryptography alerts on line 3. The line is natural-language metadata about design interviews. No weak cryptographic algorithm, cryptographic API, code execution, or secret handling is present. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only the YAML description field for the skill. The alert appears to match ordinary words such as design, not executable cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword in Instructions
low_findings.description: The static analyzer raised one weak-cryptography alert on line 6. The line instructs the assistant to ask planning questions. No weak cryptographic algorithm, cryptographic API, code execution, or secret handling is present. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 6 is plain prompt text about exploring a design decision tree. There is no code or semantic evidence of cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Low-Risk Local Codebase Inspection
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to explore the codebase when a question can be answered from project files. This is aligned with the skill purpose, but users should know it may read local project context. Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for filesystem capability, not malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly references exploring the codebase. The behavior is clear, scoped to answering design questions, and does not include exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Planning reviews often miss hidden dependencies and weak assumptions. This skill runs a structured interview that challenges decisions one question at a time.
actual_capabilities: Asks one focused question at a time about a plan or design.
Provides a recommended answer after each question.
Explores the codebase when project files can answer the question.
Follows decision branches and related dependencies.
Pushes for shared understanding before moving forward.
limitations: It does not implement changes unless the user asks separately.
It depends on clear goals and enough project context.
It may feel intensive for quick brainstorming sessions.
It cannot validate external facts without separate research.

use_cases.title: Pre-Implementation Design Review
use_cases.description: Challenge a technical plan before coding begins, including assumptions, dependencies, and unresolved decisions.

use_cases.title: Product Requirement Stress Test
use_cases.description: Question a feature plan until scope, constraints, success criteria, and tradeoffs are clear.

use_cases.title: Architecture Decision Alignment
use_cases.description: Use structured questioning to reveal risks before a team commits to an architecture direction.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Basic Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on this plan. Ask one question at a time, and give your recommended answer after each question: [paste plan].

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Stress-test this design for hidden assumptions, unclear dependencies, and weak decisions. Ask one question at a time: [paste design].

prompt_templates.title: Use The Codebase
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on this implementation plan. Inspect the codebase when a question can be answered from existing files: [paste plan].

prompt_templates.title: Resolve A Decision Tree
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk every branch of this architecture decision tree. Ask one question at a time and recommend an answer for each dependency: [paste context].

output_examples.input: Grill me on a plan to replace our authentication flow.
output: Question: What user sessions must remain valid during the migration?
Recommended answer: Keep existing sessions valid until the new flow is fully deployed and monitored.

output_examples.input: Stress-test a product requirement for a new onboarding checklist.
output: Question: Which user behavior proves the checklist is useful?
Recommended answer: Track completed setup actions and time to first successful outcome.

output_examples.input: Review an architecture plan using the codebase when needed.
output: Question: Which existing module owns this responsibility today?
Recommended answer: Inspect the relevant files first, then decide whether to extend or separate that ownership.

best_practices: Start with a short plan, goal, and known constraints.
Answer each question before moving to the next one.
Let the assistant inspect project files when code context matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you only need a quick summary.
Do not skip questions that expose uncomfortable tradeoffs.
Do not provide confidential project files unless that access is allowed.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It interviews you about a plan or design until assumptions, decisions, and dependencies are clearer.

faq.question: Does it ask multiple questions at once?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly asks one question at a time.

faq.question: Can it inspect my codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. It may inspect project files when the answer can be found in the codebase.

faq.question: Is it only for software projects?
faq.answer: No. It works for technical plans, product plans, designs, and other structured decisions.

faq.question: Will it make decisions for me?
faq.answer: It provides a recommended answer for each question, but you control the final decision.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, with only low-risk local codebase inspection documented.
